2372040.name: Medieval Darkness
2372040.description:
Medieval Darkness throws 1980s rockers into a brutal fantasy world twisted by fallen gods, undead legions, and cults that think the end of the world is a good thing.

This is a Multiplayer Online Dungeon Adventure. A MODA. That means fast progression, mission based adventures, real class roles, and persistent characters and hub towns. It also means co-op is the intended experience. You can play solo and hire followers who fill party roles, but the game truly sings(pun totally intended!) when players form a coordinated group.

Play Your Role

Classes are built for team play. Not everyone gets to be the main character. Someone has to heal you after you face-tank a skeleton warlord to impress your friends.

  • Tanks: Hold the line and control the fight

  • Healers: Keep the party alive when things go wrong

  • DPS: Make numbers go up and monsters go away

Templars can tank, heal, or attempt both at once if they enjoy living dangerously. Mages melt faces and whisk the group back to town. Rogues strike hard and vanish. Blacksmiths hit like a hammer and build the tools(and weapons, and armor) the war needs. Zealots heal by hurting themselves and everyone else questions their life choices.


Persistent Progression

You do not lose progress after a mission. Although some quests/events will reset if you do a mission again.

  • Levels stay

  • Gear stays

  • Skills stay
    Mission state reacts to wins and losses.  Complete a quest and the darkness recedes. Lose an event and the Darkness grows, making the baddies that much tougher.

  • Player plots(or player housing) is a place to build up workstations. They will go with you on certain missions.


Prestige for True Power

Once you reach level 10, you can visit a special NPC and Prestige. This resets only your character level to 1 and gives you a permanent upgrade. Gear, skills, spells, and unlocks stay with you. Some items/quests/abilities like ultimate's will require you to Prestige before you can even quest for it!

Earn Your Abilities

  • Magic and skills come from play, not from a vending machine.

  • Trainer quests: See your class trainer in Town hubs for special quests that grant your core class abilities.

  • Ultimate skills/spells will require your instrument.

  • Rare loot drops: Sometimes training books will drop that give players new skills. Sometimes you might find just the right ingredient to craft that book yourself

Build and Defend

Many missions start you off in a neighboring town that needs help… You may need to play defense, because NPCs can die. Settlements can fall. Reinforce defenses, save civilians, and survive assaults. If you slack off, the darkness grows.

Play Solo or Co-op

The game is fully playable alone with followers who fill class roles, but the intended experience is a group of players coordinating tank, healer, and DPS duties. Co-op makes every win feel earned and every mistake very public.
